description: Rules to enforce a standardized, predictable, and secure structure for all internal API routes.
philosophy: The frontend and backend must speak the same, predictable language. Every endpoint is a contract.

rules.name: Standardized JSON Response Shape
rules.description: All API endpoints MUST return a consistent JSON object structure.

requiredShape.data: T | null (The actual data payload on success)
requiredShape.error: { code: string, message: string } | null (A structured error object on failure)

rules.purpose: To allow the frontend to handle responses predictably without guessing the response structure.

rules.name: Correct HTTP Status Code Usage
rules.description: API routes MUST use appropriate HTTP status codes to reflect the outcome of the request.

guidelines.usage: Bad Request (Validation error, e.g., from Zod)
Forbidden (User is authenticated but not allowed to perform the action)

rules.name: Input Validation Mandate
rules.description: Every API route that accepts a request body or query parameters MUST validate them using a Zod schema at the beginning of the handler.
rules.purpose: To protect the backend from invalid or malicious data and provide clear 400 Bad Request errors.
